fingerprints.method_out_of_state: IdentoGO Out-of-State Live Scan (adds ~$39.95 fee) OR FD-258 hard card via mail (tip: moisturize hands for days prior to reduce smudge rejects)

seo.title: Connecticut Dietitian License Reciprocity - How to Transfer Your License (2026)
seo.description: Already licensed as a dietitian? Here's whether your license transfers to Connecticut - compact status, endorsement steps, fees, and processing time.
seo.keywords: dietitian license reciprocity connecticut, how to transfer dietitian license to connecticut, dietitian compact connecticut, connecticut dietitian endorsement application

board.name: Department of Public Health — Dietitian-Nutritionist Certification
board.mailing_address:
Connecticut Department of Public Health
Practitioner Licensing and Investigations Section
410 Capitol Ave., MS #12HLS
P.O. Box 340308
Hartford, CT 06134-0308

quick_facts.renewal_cycle: Annual + CDR 75 CPEUs per 5 years (no state-level CE mandate)

license_types.title: Certified Dietitian-Nutritionist (CDN)
license_types.description: Connecticut's credential for practicing dietitians and nutritionists. Issued by the Department of Public Health. Requires active CDR registration. As of April 2025, CDNs are eligible to enroll in Connecticut Medicaid (CMAP) and HUSKY Health.

steps.title: Obtain National Certification
steps.description: Ensure you hold current registration as a Registered Dietitian Nutritionist (RDN) or Dietetic Technician, Registered (DTR) from the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR).

steps.title: Create eLicensing Account
steps.description: Access the Connecticut eLicensing Portal at elicense.ct.gov and register for a new applicant account using your email address. Complete your user profile.

steps.title: Complete Online Application
steps.description: Log into your eLicensing account, select 'Dietitian-Nutritionist' to start the application, and accurately complete all required sections of the online form.

steps.title: Pay Application Fee
steps.description: Submit the non-refundable application fee of $50 directly through the eLicensing Portal using a credit card or electronic check.

steps.title: Submit CDR Verification
steps.description: Arrange for the Commission on Dietetic Registration (CDR) to send official verification of your current registration directly to the Connecticut Department of Public Health. This is typically done online through CDR's portal.

steps.title: Provide Additional Documentation
steps.description: Upload any other supplementary documents or information required by the eLicensing system, such as proof of name change or other requested forms, to complete your application.

documents.detail: Must be sent directly from CDR to the Connecticut DPH — self-reported copies are not accepted.
Submitted through the Connecticut eLicense online portal with the $190 fee.

provisional_text: Connecticut does not offer a provisional or temporary dietitian-nutritionist certification. Applicants must hold current CDR registration before applying. The CDR must send verification directly to DPH.
reciprocity_text: Connecticut does not have a formal endorsement or reciprocity process. Out-of-state dietitians with active CDR registration apply through the standard online process. Since Connecticut's certification is tied to CDR registration rather than state-specific exams, the process is straightforward for anyone holding an RD/RDN credential.

faqs.question: How much does a Connecticut dietitian certification cost?
faqs.answer: The initial certification fee is $190 through the eLicense portal. Annual renewal is $105. There is no state-mandated continuing education beyond maintaining your CDR registration.

faqs.question: Does Connecticut require state-level continuing education?
faqs.answer: No. Connecticut does not mandate state-specific continuing education for annual certification renewal. However, you must maintain active CDR registration, which requires 75 CPEUs every five years, including at least one CPEU in ethics or health equity.

faqs.question: Can Connecticut CDNs enroll in Medicaid?
faqs.answer: Yes. Effective April 1, 2025, certified dietitian-nutritionists can enroll in Connecticut's Medicaid program (CMAP) and HUSKY Health. Reimbursement for services began on July 1, 2025.

faqs.question: Does Connecticut have reciprocity for dietitians?
faqs.answer: No interstate compact currently covers dietitian practice in Connecticut. To work in Connecticut, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

faqs.question: How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Connecticut?
faqs.answer: Most endorsement applications take 4 to 6 Weeks.

faqs.question: Can I start working in Connecticut while my endorsement application is processing?
faqs.answer: Most applicants need to wait for full approval before starting work because the state does not clearly offer a temporary or provisional credential.

reciprocity.notes: Connecticut does not have a formal endorsement or reciprocity process. Out-of-state dietitians with active CDR registration apply through the standard online process. Since Connecticut's certification is tied to CDR registration rather than state-specific exams, the process is straightforward for anyone holding an RD/RDN credential.

hero.title: Become a Dietitian in Connecticut
hero.dek: To apply for a Dietitian-Nutritionist license in Connecticut, submit your application and all supporting documentation through the state's official eLicensing Portal.

official_links.registration_steps: Access the eLicensing Portal. Select 'New Applicants' to register for an account using your email address. Follow the prompts to complete your user profile. Once registered, log in and select 'Dietitian-Nutritionist' from the list of professions to begin your online application.
